tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows impaired renal function (elevated creatinine, proteinuria, low urine specific gravity), mild hyperkalemia, and significant ketonuria. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) may suggest dehydration or possible urinary tract pathology, though no infection markers are present. Glucose and HbA1c are low, indicating no hyperglycemia. Recommend further evaluation of renal function and possible causes of ketonuria.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
